motivation: The Qwen2-7B causality experiment (poc_layer0_causality) showed 99.97% rescue by fixing Layer 0 alone. Question: does this Layer-0-dominant pattern generalize across different failure types, or is it specific to Qwen2-7B's catastrophic case? Test three additional models spanning severity levels.

pythia_6_9b.note: Fixing Layer 0 alone rescues only 0.1% of the damage (+7.47 vs +7.48). Conversely, breaking Layer 0 alone leaves ~full rescue (+0.82 vs +0.84 all_nsep). Layer 0 contributes essentially nothing to Pythia-6.9B's quantization damage -- the damage is distributed across all 32 layers as cumulative norm-variation error. Outlier ratio 4.6x consistent with Mode 2. Confirms the Pythia family's failure is qualitatively different from Qwen2-7B's.

qwen2_5_14b.note: Alibaba's Qwen2 -> Qwen2.5 recipe revision substantially resolved the Layer 0 outlier pathology of Qwen2-7B; what remains is mild distributed damage (+0.185) of Mode 2 type. Layer 0 fix rescues only 4.3%. This shows the same vendor's recipe can move between modes: the Layer 0 pathology of Qwen2-7B is not a universal Qwen family trait but a specific Qwen2 artifact.

pythia_12b.note: Same pattern as Pythia-6.9B but stronger absolute damage (+14.82 vs +7.48). Fix L0 rescue 0.3%, break L0 contribution ~0.08%. Pythia family uniformly exhibits Mode 2 failure across scales.

Mode_1_outlier_driven.causal_test: fixing Layer 0 alone rescues >99% of damage
Mode_1_outlier_driven.mechanism: single channel dominates L0 per-row absmax; errors propagate multiplicatively

Mode_2_norm_variation_driven.signature: K_max at Layer 0 < 10x; damage distributed across all layers
Mode_2_norm_variation_driven.causal_test: fixing Layer 0 alone rescues < 5% of damage
Mode_2_norm_variation_driven.mechanism: per-token norm variation accumulates additively as each layer's per-row scale fails to fit the dynamic range

paper_implication: This is the first empirical separation of two distinct KV-quantization failure modes via a causal intervention. The modes were theoretically introduced in Section 4 of the paper as separate failure causes, and this experiment isolates them: Mode 1 is Layer-0-localized (Qwen2-7B), Mode 2 is distributed (all other failing models). nsep+pchan4's superlinear improvement (744x on Qwen2-7B) is the conjunction of two methods each addressing one mode -- norm separation handles Mode 2, per-channel quantization handles Mode 1. The method works universally because it covers both modes.
practical_diagnostic: Measure K_max at Layer 0. If >= 15x and concentrated, suspected Mode 1 case -- Layer 0 selective compression is sufficient. Otherwise, suspected Mode 2 (or no meaningful damage) -- uniform nsep+pchan4 is required for any observed damage. Uniform nsep+pchan4 handles both modes and is the safe default when the profile cannot be audited.
